title: Qlog Core Model and Serialization Envelope (Draft Main Schema)
purpose: Capture the draft qlog main-schema requirements for Incursa.Qlog as the basis for a serialization-independent core model and trace envelope.
scope: This specification covers the qlog file envelope, trace and trace-sequence metadata, event identity, common fields, schema-URI rules, and JSON or JSON Text Sequences serialization behavior needed for a stable core library baseline. It intentionally leaves HTTP/3 and other application-specific adapters to later work.
context: Source material is draft-ietf-quic-qlog-main-schema v13, which is still a work-in-progress document. The requirements below preserve draft provenance and should be revisited if the draft changes.
open_questions: Should future protocol vocabularies continue to share the core qlog model package boundary or follow the sibling-package pattern used for QUIC and CBOR?

requirements.title: Declare qlog file metadata
requirements.statement: A qlog file MUST declare a file schema URI and a serialization format media type.

requirements.title: Carry component traces or trace errors in contained files
requirements.statement: A contained qlog file MUST carry component traces or explicit trace conversion errors.

requirements.title: Carry a single trace header in sequential files
requirements.statement: A sequential qlog file MUST carry a single trace header whose events are appended after it.

requirements.title: Preserve trace metadata and shared fields
requirements.statement: A qlog trace MUST expose event_schemas and preserve shared trace context through common_fields and vantage_point.

requirements.title: Record each event as time, name, and data
requirements.statement: A qlog event MUST record time, name, and data.

requirements.title: Use namespace-qualified event names
requirements.statement: A qlog event name MUST combine the namespace identifier, a colon, and the event type identifier.

requirements.title: Register absolute event schema URIs
requirements.statement: An event schema URI MUST be absolute and include the namespace identifier.

requirements.title: Extend the protocol event data type
requirements.statement: An event data definition MUST extend ProtocolEventData when it adds new qlog events.

requirements.title: Ignore unknown common fields and advisory schema listings
requirements.statement: A qlog implementation MUST ignore unknown entries in common_fields and treat event_schemas as advisory rather than exhaustive.

requirements.title: Preserve private and extension event schema URIs
requirements.statement: A qlog implementation MUST accept and preserve absolute private or extension event schema URIs as opaque identifiers during serialize and deserialize round trips, even when the URI is not part of the built-in schema registry.

requirements.title: Expose contained JSON stream serializer entry points
requirements.statement: The contained qlog serializer MUST expose string and Stream serialization entry points so callers can write contained qlog output directly to files, network streams, or memory buffers without materializing intermediate text.

requirements.title: Expose sequential JSON stream serializer entry points
requirements.statement: The sequential qlog serializer MUST expose string and Stream serialization entry points so callers can write sequential qlog output directly to files, network streams, or memory buffers without materializing intermediate text.

requirements.title: Lowercase JSON Text Sequence field names
requirements.statement: A JSON Text Sequences qlog file MUST serialize qlog field names in lowercase.
